comments.text: With the roll back of clean drinking water rules around PFAS, it’s time to start banging pots and pans about the impact in Michigan. I teamed up with Great Lakes PFAS Action for a town hall in Oscoda to hear directly from folks living with the impact of PFAS contamination.
PFAS levels in #GreatLakes fish are dropping, study finds The declining levels were driven by an industry phase-out of legacy PFAS chemicals www.wpr.org/news/pfas-le...

Michigan was one of the first to issue PFAS warnings about the foam and the dangers of swimming or eating the fish. It's why I teamed up with Great Lakes PFAS Action for a town hall in Oscoda. This isn’t partisan, it’s about clean water.

The folks in Marinette have been fighting PFAS contamination for years, including at Rennoe Park, right on Lake Michigan. I'm glad we'll be getting over $125 million out the door to help communities like theirs.

just because it looks clear doesn't mean it is (a) clean or (b) a healthy ecosystem. sterile ecosystem = dead ecosystem also please see the Great Lakes, where over the past decades invasive bivalves have been similarly "cleaning" water and in general wreaking havoc on native habitats and species

Incense, hash, Hai Karate (ugh), mimeograph paper, skunk weed, gasoline at pump, Djarum and Kretek, tobacco, Lake Erie sewage, and airplane glue (never sniffed, but man did I get high as a kite making a WWII Spitfire model in an unventilated basement). But you could see the stars.

🦅 Baby bald eagles in the Great Lakes are testing positive for toxic chemicals like PFAS, and long-banned PCBs and DDT. But there’s also hope: a new soybean-based product may soon replace cancer-causing firefighter foam. For more on the latest PFAS news:
www.greatlakesnow.org/2025/07/baby...

In the Great Lakes State, water is vital to our way of life. Unfortunately, PFAS contamination is threatening Michigan’s health and well-being, and even our economy. That’s why I wrote a bill to keep our water clean and free of pollutants. Check it out:
michiganadvance.com/briefs/steve...

Lead in gasoline, causing mental acuity issues in kids; Lake Erie on fire; floating sludge in the Hudson River; eagles on the brink of extinction because of DDT in the environment; asbestos in everything -- I remember.
No more PFAS in the water. Michiganders deserve better. My new bipartisan bill will upgrade our aging water systems and protect our Great Lakes.

28-Year review of acute toxicity in rainbow trout and Daphnia magna exposed to industrial effluents in Ontario, Canada #GreatLakes 🧪 www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti... pollution reduction led to a significant decrease in acute toxicity in Ontario industrial effluents

PFAS contamination has impacted communities across Michigan, and families deserve clean, safe water.
I appreciated the conversation today with NRDC and local advocates about protecting the Great Lakes, strengthening water infrastructure, and making sure polluters are held accountable.
